            Line data    Source code
       1              : /*-------------------------------------------------------------------------
       2              :  *
       3              :  * plancache.c
       4              :  *        Plan cache management.
       5              :  *
       6              :  * The plan cache manager has two principal responsibilities: deciding when
       7              :  * to use a generic plan versus a custom (parameter-value-specific) plan,
       8              :  * and tracking whether cached plans need to be invalidated because of schema
       9              :  * changes in the objects they depend on.
      10              :  *
      11              :  * The logic for choosing generic or custom plans is in choose_custom_plan,
      12              :  * which see for comments.
      13              :  *
      14              :  * Cache invalidation is driven off sinval events.  Any CachedPlanSource
      15              :  * that matches the event is marked invalid, as is its generic CachedPlan
      16              :  * if it has one.  When (and if) the next demand for a cached plan occurs,
      17              :  * parse analysis and rewrite is repeated to build a new valid query tree,
      18              :  * and then planning is performed as normal.  We also force re-analysis and
      19              :  * re-planning if the active search_path is different from the previous time
      20              :  * or, if RLS is involved, if the user changes or the RLS environment changes.
      21              :  *
      22              :  * Note that if the sinval was a result of user DDL actions, parse analysis
      23              :  * could throw an error, for example if a column referenced by the query is
      24              :  * no longer present.  Another possibility is for the query's output tupdesc
      25              :  * to change (for instance "SELECT *" might expand differently than before).
      26              :  * The creator of a cached plan can specify whether it is allowable for the
      27              :  * query to change output tupdesc on replan --- if so, it's up to the
      28              :  * caller to notice changes and cope with them.
      29              :  *
      30              :  * Currently, we track exactly the dependencies of plans on relations,
      31              :  * user-defined functions, and domains.  On relcache invalidation events or
      32              :  * pg_proc or pg_type syscache invalidation events, we invalidate just those
      33              :  * plans that depend on the particular object being modified.  (Note: this
      34              :  * scheme assumes that any table modification that requires replanning will
      35              :  * generate a relcache inval event.)  We also watch for inval events on
      36              :  * certain other system catalogs, such as pg_namespace; but for them, our
      37              :  * response is just to invalidate all plans.  We expect updates on those
      38              :  * catalogs to be infrequent enough that more-detailed tracking is not worth
      39              :  * the effort.
      40              :  *
      41              :  * In addition to full-fledged query plans, we provide a facility for
      42              :  * detecting invalidations of simple scalar expressions.  This is fairly
      43              :  * bare-bones; it's the caller's responsibility to build a new expression
      44              :  * if the old one gets invalidated.
      45              :  *
      46              :  *
      47              :  * Portions Copyright (c) 1996-2020, PostgreSQL Global Development Group
      48              :  * Portions Copyright (c) 1994, Regents of the University of California
      49              :  *
      50              :  * IDENTIFICATION
      51              :  *        src/backend/utils/cache/plancache.c
      52              :  *
      53              :  *-------------------------------------------------------------------------
      54              :  */
      55              : #include "postgres.h"
      56              :

      60              : #include "catalog/namespace.h"

      78              : /*
      79              :  * We must skip "overhead" operations that involve database access when the
      80              :  * cached plan's subject statement is a transaction control command or one
      81              :  * that requires a snapshot not to be set yet (such as SET or LOCK).  More
      82              :  * generally, statements that do not require parse analysis/rewrite/plan
      83              :  * activity never need to be revalidated, so we can treat them all like that.
      84              :  * For the convenience of postgres.c, treat empty statements that way too.
      85              :  */
      86              : #define StmtPlanRequiresRevalidation(plansource)  \
      87              :         ((plansource)->raw_parse_tree != NULL && \
      88              :          stmt_requires_parse_analysis((plansource)->raw_parse_tree))
      89              :

      92              :  * those that are in long-lived storage and are examined for sinval events).
      93              :  * We use a dlist instead of separate List cells so that we can guarantee
      94              :  * to save a CachedPlanSource without error.
      95              :  */
      96              : static dlist_head saved_plan_list = DLIST_STATIC_INIT(saved_plan_list);

     142              : 
     143              : /*
     144              :  * CreateCachedPlan: initially create a plan cache entry.
     145              :  *
     146              :  * Creation of a cached plan is divided into two steps, CreateCachedPlan and
     147              :  * CompleteCachedPlan.  CreateCachedPlan should be called after running the
     148              :  * query through raw_parser, but before doing parse analysis and rewrite;
     149              :  * CompleteCachedPlan is called after that.  The reason for this arrangement
     150              :  * is that it can save one round of copying of the raw parse tree, since
     151              :  * the parser will normally scribble on the raw parse tree.  Callers would
     152              :  * otherwise need to make an extra copy of the parse tree to ensure they
     153              :  * still had a clean copy to present at plan cache creation time.
     154              :  *
     155              :  * All arguments presented to CreateCachedPlan are copied into a memory
     156              :  * context created as a child of the call-time CurrentMemoryContext, which
     157              :  * should be a reasonably short-lived working context that will go away in
     158              :  * event of an error.  This ensures that the cached plan data structure will
     159              :  * likewise disappear if an error occurs before we have fully constructed it.
     160              :  * Once constructed, the cached plan can be made longer-lived, if needed,
     161              :  * by calling SaveCachedPlan.
     162              :  *
     163              :  * raw_parse_tree: output of raw_parser(), or NULL if empty query
     164              :  * query_string: original query text
     165              :  * commandTag: command tag for query, or UNKNOWN if empty query
     166              :  */
     167              : CachedPlanSource *
     168           30 : CreateCachedPlan(RawStmt *raw_parse_tree,
     169              :                                  const char *query_string,
     170              :                                  CommandTag commandTag)
     171              : {
     172              :         CachedPlanSource *plansource;
     173              :         MemoryContext source_context;
     174              :         MemoryContext oldcxt;
     175              : 
     176              :         Assert(query_string != NULL);   /* required as of 8.4 */
     177              : 
     178              :         /*
     179              :          * Make a dedicated memory context for the CachedPlanSource and its
     180              :          * permanent subsidiary data.  It's probably not going to be large, but
     181              :          * just in case, allow it to grow large.  Initially it's a child of the
     182              :          * caller's context (which we assume to be transient), so that it will be
     183              :          * cleaned up on error.
     184              :          */
     185           30 :         source_context = AllocSetContextCreate(CurrentMemoryContext,
     186              :                                                                                    "CachedPlanSource",
     187              :                                                                                    ALLOCSET_START_SMALL_SIZES);
     188              : 
     189              :         /*
     190              :          * Create and fill the CachedPlanSource struct within the new context.
     191              :          * Most fields are just left empty for the moment.
     192              :          */
     193           30 :         oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(source_context);
     194              : 
     195           30 :         plansource = (CachedPlanSource *) palloc0(sizeof(CachedPlanSource));
     196           30 :         plansource->magic = CACHEDPLANSOURCE_MAGIC;
     197           30 :         plansource->raw_parse_tree = copyObject(raw_parse_tree);
     198           30 :         plansource->query_string = pstrdup(query_string);
     199           30 :         MemoryContextSetIdentifier(source_context, plansource->query_string);
     200           30 :         plansource->commandTag = commandTag;
     201           30 :         plansource->param_types = NULL;
     202           30 :         plansource->num_params = 0;
     203           30 :         plansource->parserSetup = NULL;
     204           30 :         plansource->parserSetupArg = NULL;
     205           30 :         plansource->cursor_options = 0;
     206           30 :         plansource->fixed_result = false;
     207           30 :         plansource->resultDesc = NULL;
     208           30 :         plansource->context = source_context;
     209           30 :         plansource->query_list = NIL;
     210           30 :         plansource->relationOids = NIL;
     211           30 :         plansource->invalItems = NIL;
     212           30 :         plansource->search_path = NULL;
     213           30 :         plansource->query_context = NULL;
     214           30 :         plansource->rewriteRoleId = InvalidOid;
     215           30 :         plansource->rewriteRowSecurity = false;
     216           30 :         plansource->dependsOnRLS = false;
     217           30 :         plansource->gplan = NULL;
     218           30 :         plansource->is_oneshot = false;
     219           30 :         plansource->is_complete = false;
     220           30 :         plansource->is_saved = false;
     221           30 :         plansource->is_valid = false;
     222           30 :         plansource->generation = 0;
     223           30 :         plansource->generic_cost = -1;
     224           30 :         plansource->total_custom_cost = 0;
     225           30 :         plansource->num_custom_plans = 0;
     226              : 
     227           30 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
     228              : 
     229           30 :         return plansource;
     230              : }
     231              : 
     232              : /*
     233              :  * CreateOneShotCachedPlan: initially create a one-shot plan cache entry.
     234              :  *
     235              :  * This variant of CreateCachedPlan creates a plan cache entry that is meant
     236              :  * to be used only once.  No data copying occurs: all data structures remain
     237              :  * in the caller's memory context (which typically should get cleared after
     238              :  * completing execution).  The CachedPlanSource struct itself is also created
     239              :  * in that context.
     240              :  *
     241              :  * A one-shot plan cannot be saved or copied, since we make no effort to
     242              :  * preserve the raw parse tree unmodified.  There is also no support for
     243              :  * invalidation, so plan use must be completed in the current transaction,
     244              :  * and DDL that might invalidate the querytree_list must be avoided as well.
     245              :  *
     246              :  * raw_parse_tree: output of raw_parser(), or NULL if empty query
     247              :  * query_string: original query text
     248              :  * commandTag: command tag for query, or NULL if empty query
     249              :  */
     250              : CachedPlanSource *
     251            9 : CreateOneShotCachedPlan(RawStmt *raw_parse_tree,
     252              :                                                 const char *query_string,
     253              :                                                 CommandTag commandTag)
     254              : {
     255              :         CachedPlanSource *plansource;
     256              : 
     257              :         Assert(query_string != NULL);   /* required as of 8.4 */
     258              : 
     259              :         /*
     260              :          * Create and fill the CachedPlanSource struct within the caller's memory
     261              :          * context.  Most fields are just left empty for the moment.
     262              :          */
     263            9 :         plansource = (CachedPlanSource *) palloc0(sizeof(CachedPlanSource));
     264            9 :         plansource->magic = CACHEDPLANSOURCE_MAGIC;
     265            9 :         plansource->raw_parse_tree = raw_parse_tree;
     266            9 :         plansource->query_string = query_string;
     267            9 :         plansource->commandTag = commandTag;
     268            9 :         plansource->param_types = NULL;
     269            9 :         plansource->num_params = 0;
     270            9 :         plansource->parserSetup = NULL;
     271            9 :         plansource->parserSetupArg = NULL;
     272            9 :         plansource->cursor_options = 0;
     273            9 :         plansource->fixed_result = false;
     274            9 :         plansource->resultDesc = NULL;
     275            9 :         plansource->context = CurrentMemoryContext;
     276            9 :         plansource->query_list = NIL;
     277            9 :         plansource->relationOids = NIL;
     278            9 :         plansource->invalItems = NIL;
     279            9 :         plansource->search_path = NULL;
     280            9 :         plansource->query_context = NULL;
     281            9 :         plansource->rewriteRoleId = InvalidOid;
     282            9 :         plansource->rewriteRowSecurity = false;
     283            9 :         plansource->dependsOnRLS = false;
     284            9 :         plansource->gplan = NULL;
     285            9 :         plansource->is_oneshot = true;
     286            9 :         plansource->is_complete = false;
     287            9 :         plansource->is_saved = false;
     288            9 :         plansource->is_valid = false;
     289            9 :         plansource->generation = 0;
     290            9 :         plansource->generic_cost = -1;
     291            9 :         plansource->total_custom_cost = 0;
     292            9 :         plansource->num_custom_plans = 0;
     293              : 
     294            9 :         return plansource;
     295              : }
     296              : 
     297              : /*
     298              :  * CompleteCachedPlan: second step of creating a plan cache entry.
     299              :  *
     300              :  * Pass in the analyzed-and-rewritten form of the query, as well as the
     301              :  * required subsidiary data about parameters and such.  All passed values will
     302              :  * be copied into the CachedPlanSource's memory, except as specified below.
     303              :  * After this is called, GetCachedPlan can be called to obtain a plan, and
     304              :  * optionally the CachedPlanSource can be saved using SaveCachedPlan.
     305              :  *
     306              :  * If querytree_context is not NULL, the querytree_list must be stored in that
     307              :  * context (but the other parameters need not be).  The querytree_list is not
     308              :  * copied, rather the given context is kept as the initial query_context of
     309              :  * the CachedPlanSource.  (It should have been created as a child of the
     310              :  * caller's working memory context, but it will now be reparented to belong
     311              :  * to the CachedPlanSource.)  The querytree_context is normally the context in
     312              :  * which the caller did raw parsing and parse analysis.  This approach saves
     313              :  * one tree copying step compared to passing NULL, but leaves lots of extra
     314              :  * cruft in the query_context, namely whatever extraneous stuff parse analysis
     315              :  * created, as well as whatever went unused from the raw parse tree.  Using
     316              :  * this option is a space-for-time tradeoff that is appropriate if the
     317              :  * CachedPlanSource is not expected to survive long.
     318              :  *
     319              :  * plancache.c cannot know how to copy the data referenced by parserSetupArg,
     320              :  * and it would often be inappropriate to do so anyway.  When using that
     321              :  * option, it is caller's responsibility that the referenced data remains
     322              :  * valid for as long as the CachedPlanSource exists.
     323              :  *
     324              :  * If the CachedPlanSource is a "oneshot" plan, then no querytree copying
     325              :  * occurs at all, and querytree_context is ignored; it is caller's
     326              :  * responsibility that the passed querytree_list is sufficiently long-lived.
     327              :  *
     328              :  * plansource: structure returned by CreateCachedPlan
     329              :  * querytree_list: analyzed-and-rewritten form of query (list of Query nodes)
     330              :  * querytree_context: memory context containing querytree_list,
     331              :  *                                        or NULL to copy querytree_list into a fresh context
     332              :  * param_types: array of fixed parameter type OIDs, or NULL if none
     333              :  * num_params: number of fixed parameters
     334              :  * parserSetup: alternate method for handling query parameters
     335              :  * parserSetupArg: data to pass to parserSetup
     336              :  * cursor_options: options bitmask to pass to planner
     337              :  * fixed_result: true to disallow future changes in query's result tupdesc
     338              :  */
     339              : void
     340           39 : CompleteCachedPlan(CachedPlanSource *plansource,
     341              :                                    List *querytree_list,
     342              :                                    MemoryContext querytree_context,
     343              :                                    Oid *param_types,
     344              :                                    int num_params,
     345              :                                    ParserSetupHook parserSetup,
     346              :                                    void *parserSetupArg,
     347              :                                    int cursor_options,
     348              :                                    bool fixed_result)
     349              : {
     350           39 :         MemoryContext source_context = plansource->context;
     351           39 :         MemoryContext oldcxt = CurrentMemoryContext;
     352              : 
     353              :         /* Assert caller is doing things in a sane order */
     354              :         Assert(plansource->magic == CACHEDPLANSOURCE_MAGIC);
     355              :         Assert(!plansource->is_complete);
     356              : 
     357              :         /*
     358              :          * If caller supplied a querytree_context, reparent it underneath the
     359              :          * CachedPlanSource's context; otherwise, create a suitable context and
     360              :          * copy the querytree_list into it.  But no data copying should be done
     361              :          * for one-shot plans; for those, assume the passed querytree_list is
     362              :          * sufficiently long-lived.
     363              :          */
     364           39 :         if (plansource->is_oneshot)
     365              :         {
     366            9 :                 querytree_context = CurrentMemoryContext;
     367              :         }
     368           30 :         else if (querytree_context != NULL)
     369              :         {
     370            0 :                 MemoryContextSetParent(querytree_context, source_context);
     371            0 :                 MemoryContextSwitchTo(querytree_context);
     372              :         }
     373              :         else
     374              :         {
     375              :                 /* Again, it's a good bet the querytree_context can be small */
     376           30 :                 querytree_context = AllocSetContextCreate(source_context,
     377              :                                                                                                   "CachedPlanQuery",
     378              :                                                                                                   ALLOCSET_START_SMALL_SIZES);
     379           30 :                 MemoryContextSwitchTo(querytree_context);
     380           30 :                 querytree_list = copyObject(querytree_list);
     381              :         }
     382              : 
     383           39 :         plansource->query_context = querytree_context;
     384           39 :         plansource->query_list = querytree_list;
     385              : 
     386           39 :         if (!plansource->is_oneshot && StmtPlanRequiresRevalidation(plansource))
     387              :         {
     388              :                 /*
     389              :                  * Use the planner machinery to extract dependencies.  Data is saved
     390              :                  * in query_context.  (We assume that not a lot of extra cruft is
     391              :                  * created by this call.)  We can skip this for one-shot plans, and
     392              :                  * plans not needing revalidation have no such dependencies anyway.
     393              :                  */
     394           30 :                 extract_query_dependencies((Node *) querytree_list,
     395              :                                                                    &plansource->relationOids,
     396              :                                                                    &plansource->invalItems,
     397              :                                                                    &plansource->dependsOnRLS);
     398              : 
     399              :                 /* Update RLS info as well. */
     400           30 :                 plansource->rewriteRoleId = GetUserId();
     401           30 :                 plansource->rewriteRowSecurity = row_security;
     402              : 
     403              :                 /*
     404              :                  * Also save the current search_path in the query_context.  (This
     405              :                  * should not generate much extra cruft either, since almost certainly
     406              :                  * the path is already valid.)  Again, we don't really need this for
     407              :                  * one-shot plans; and we *must* skip this for transaction control
     408              :                  * commands, because this could result in catalog accesses.
     409              :                  */
     410           30 :                 plansource->search_path = GetOverrideSearchPath(querytree_context);
     411              :         }
     412              : 
     413              :         /*
     414              :          * Save the final parameter types (or other parameter specification data)
     415              :          * into the source_context, as well as our other parameters.  Also save
     416              :          * the result tuple descriptor.
     417              :          */
     418           39 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(source_context);
     419              : 
     420           39 :         if (num_params > 0)
     421              :         {
     422            8 :                 plansource->param_types = (Oid *) palloc(num_params * sizeof(Oid));
     423            8 :                 memcpy(plansource->param_types, param_types, num_params * sizeof(Oid));
     424              :         }
     425              :         else
     426           31 :                 plansource->param_types = NULL;
     427           39 :         plansource->num_params = num_params;
     428           39 :         plansource->parserSetup = parserSetup;
     429           39 :         plansource->parserSetupArg = parserSetupArg;
     430           39 :         plansource->cursor_options = cursor_options;
     431           39 :         plansource->fixed_result = fixed_result;
     432           39 :         plansource->resultDesc = PlanCacheComputeResultDesc(querytree_list);
     433              : 
     434           39 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
     435              : 
     436           39 :         plansource->is_complete = true;
     437           39 :         plansource->is_valid = true;
     438           39 : }
     439              : 
     440              : /*
     441              :  * SaveCachedPlan: save a cached plan permanently
     442              :  *
     443              :  * This function moves the cached plan underneath CacheMemoryContext (making
     444              :  * it live for the life of the backend, unless explicitly dropped), and adds
     445              :  * it to the list of cached plans that are checked for invalidation when an
     446              :  * sinval event occurs.
     447              :  *
     448              :  * This is guaranteed not to throw error, except for the caller-error case
     449              :  * of trying to save a one-shot plan.  Callers typically depend on that
     450              :  * since this is called just before or just after adding a pointer to the
     451              :  * CachedPlanSource to some permanent data structure of their own.  Up until
     452              :  * this is done, a CachedPlanSource is just transient data that will go away
     453              :  * automatically on transaction abort.
     454              :  */
     455              : void
     456           12 : SaveCachedPlan(CachedPlanSource *plansource)
     457              : {
     458              :         /* Assert caller is doing things in a sane order */
     459              :         Assert(plansource->magic == CACHEDPLANSOURCE_MAGIC);
     460              :         Assert(plansource->is_complete);
     461              :         Assert(!plansource->is_saved);
     462              : 
     463              :         /* This seems worth a real test, though */
     464           12 :         if (plansource->is_oneshot)
     465            0 :                 elog(ERROR, "cannot save one-shot cached plan");

     467              :         /*

     469              :          * plans from the CachedPlanSource.  If there is a generic plan, moving it

     476              :         /*

     491              : /*

     541              : 
     542              : /*
     543              :  * RevalidateCachedQuery: ensure validity of analyzed-and-rewritten query tree.
     544              :  *
     545              :  * What we do here is re-acquire locks and redo parse analysis if necessary.
     546              :  * On return, the query_list is valid and we have sufficient locks to begin
     547              :  * planning.
     548              :  *
     549              :  * If any parse analysis activity is required, the caller's memory context is
     550              :  * used for that work.
     551              :  *

     553              :  * had to do re-analysis, and NIL otherwise.  (This is returned just to save
     554              :  * a tree copying step in a subsequent BuildCachedPlan call.)

     557           67 : RevalidateCachedQuery(CachedPlanSource *plansource,
     558              :                                           QueryEnvironment *queryEnv)
     559              : {

     562              :         List       *tlist;                      /* transient query-tree list */

     565              :         MemoryContext querytree_context;
     566              :         MemoryContext oldcxt;
     567              : 
     568              :         /*
     569              :          * For one-shot plans, we do not support revalidation checking; it's
     570              :          * assumed the query is parsed, planned, and executed in one transaction,

     572              :          * type can't require revalidation, we needn't do anything (and we mustn't

     574              :          */

     577              :                 Assert(plansource->is_valid);
     578            9 :                 return NIL;
     579              :         }
     580              : 
     581              :         /*

     596              :         }
     597              : 
     598              :         /*

     603            0 :                 (plansource->rewriteRoleId != GetUserId() ||
     604            0 :                  plansource->rewriteRowSecurity != row_security))

     606              : 
     607              :         /*
     608              :          * If the query is currently valid, acquire locks on the referenced

     613              :         {
     614           53 :                 AcquirePlannerLocks(plansource->query_list, true);
     615              : 
     616              :                 /*
     617              :                  * By now, if any invalidation has happened, the inval callback

     619              :                  */
     620           53 :                 if (plansource->is_valid)
     621              :                 {
     622              :                         /* Successfully revalidated and locked the query. */
     623           53 :                         return NIL;
     624              :                 }
     625              : 
     626              :                 /* Oops, the race case happened.  Release useless locks. */
     627            0 :                 AcquirePlannerLocks(plansource->query_list, false);
     628              :         }
     629              : 
     630              :         /*

     636            5 :         plansource->query_list = NIL;
     637            5 :         plansource->relationOids = NIL;

     639            5 :         plansource->search_path = NULL;

     787              : /*
     788              :  * CheckCachedPlan: see if the CachedPlanSource's generic plan is valid.

     791              :  * querytree is up to date.

     797           36 : CheckCachedPlan(CachedPlanSource *plansource)

     800              : 
     801              :         /* Assert that caller checked the querytree */

     804              :         /* If there's no generic plan, just say "false" */

     808              :         Assert(plan->magic == CACHEDPLAN_MAGIC);

     812              :         /*

     864              : /*

     869              :  *
     870              :  * To build a generic, parameter-value-independent plan, pass NULL for
     871              :  * boundParams.  To build a custom plan, pass the actual parameter values via
     872              :  * boundParams.  For best effect, the PARAM_FLAG_CONST flag should be set on
     873              :  * each parameter value; otherwise the planner will treat the value as a
     874              :  * hint rather than a hard constant.

     876              :  * Planning work is done in the caller's memory context.  The finished plan

     940              :         /* Release snapshot if we got one */

     944              :         /*

     966              : 
     967              :         /*

     989           51 :                 if (plannedstmt->transientPlan)

    1000           56 :                 plan->saved_xmin = InvalidTransactionId;
    1001           56 :         plan->refcount = 0;
    1002           56 :         plan->context = plan_context;
    1003           56 :         plan->is_oneshot = plansource->is_oneshot;
    1004           56 :         plan->is_saved = false;
    1005           56 :         plan->is_valid = true;
    1006              : 
    1007              :         /* assign generation number to new plan */
    1008           56 :         plan->generation = ++(plansource->generation);

    1012           56 :         return plan;

    1015              : /*
    1016              :  * choose_custom_plan: choose whether to use custom or generic plan
    1017              :  *
    1018              :  * This defines the policy followed by GetCachedPlan.
    1019              :  */
    1020              : static bool
    1021           92 : choose_custom_plan(CachedPlanSource *plansource, ParamListInfo boundParams)
    1022              : {
    1023              :         double          avg_custom_cost;
    1024              : 
    1025              :         /* One-shot plans will always be considered custom */
    1026           92 :         if (plansource->is_oneshot)
    1027            9 :                 return true;
    1028              : 
    1029              :         /* Otherwise, never any point in a custom plan if there's no parameters */
    1030           83 :         if (boundParams == NULL)
    1031           44 :                 return false;
    1032              :         /* ... nor when planning would be a no-op */
    1033           39 :         if (!StmtPlanRequiresRevalidation(plansource))
    1034            0 :                 return false;
    1035              : 
    1036              :         /* Let settings force the decision */
    1037           39 :         if (plan_cache_mode == PLAN_CACHE_MODE_FORCE_GENERIC_PLAN)
    1038            0 :                 return false;
    1039           39 :         if (plan_cache_mode == PLAN_CACHE_MODE_FORCE_CUSTOM_PLAN)
    1040            0 :                 return true;
    1041              : 
    1042              :         /* See if caller wants to force the decision */
    1043           39 :         if (plansource->cursor_options & CURSOR_OPT_GENERIC_PLAN)
    1044            0 :                 return false;
    1045           39 :         if (plansource->cursor_options & CURSOR_OPT_CUSTOM_PLAN)
    1046            0 :                 return true;
    1047              : 
    1048              :         /* Generate custom plans until we have done at least 5 (arbitrary) */
    1049           39 :         if (plansource->num_custom_plans < 5)
    1050           22 :                 return true;
    1051              : 
    1052           17 :         avg_custom_cost = plansource->total_custom_cost / plansource->num_custom_plans;
    1053              : 
    1054              :         /*
    1055              :          * Prefer generic plan if it's less expensive than the average custom
    1056              :          * plan.  (Because we include a charge for cost of planning in the
    1057              :          * custom-plan costs, this means the generic plan only has to be less
    1058              :          * expensive than the execution cost plus replan cost of the custom
    1059              :          * plans.)

    1062              :          * the generic plan cost), we'll always prefer generic at this point.
    1063              :          */
    1064           17 :         if (plansource->generic_cost < avg_custom_cost)
    1065           17 :                 return false;
    1066              : 
    1067            0 :         return true;
    1068              : }
    1069              : 
    1070              : /*
    1071              :  * cached_plan_cost: calculate estimated cost of a plan
    1072              :  *
    1073              :  * If include_planner is true, also include the estimated cost of constructing
    1074              :  * the plan.  (We must factor that into the cost of using a custom plan, but
    1075              :  * we don't count it for a generic plan.)
    1076              :  */
    1077              : static double
    1078           56 : cached_plan_cost(CachedPlan *plan, bool include_planner)
    1079              : {
    1080           56 :         double          result = 0;
    1081              :         ListCell   *lc;
    1082              : 
    1083          112 :         foreach(lc, plan->stmt_list)
    1084              :         {
    1085           56 :                 PlannedStmt *plannedstmt = lfirst_node(PlannedStmt, lc);
    1086              : 
    1087           56 :                 if (plannedstmt->commandType == CMD_UTILITY)
    1088            5 :                         continue;                       /* Ignore utility statements */
    1089              : 
    1090           51 :                 result += plannedstmt->planTree->total_cost;
    1091              : 
    1092           51 :                 if (include_planner)
    1093              :                 {
    1094              :                         /*
    1095              :                          * Currently we use a very crude estimate of planning effort based
    1096              :                          * on the number of relations in the finished plan's rangetable.
    1097              :                          * Join planning effort actually scales much worse than linearly
    1098              :                          * in the number of relations --- but only until the join collapse
    1099              :                          * limits kick in.  Also, while inheritance child relations surely
    1100              :                          * add to planning effort, they don't make the join situation
    1101              :                          * worse.  So the actual shape of the planning cost curve versus
    1102              :                          * number of relations isn't all that obvious.  It will take
    1103              :                          * considerable work to arrive at a less crude estimate, and for
    1104              :                          * now it's not clear that's worth doing.
    1105              :                          *
    1106              :                          * The other big difficulty here is that we don't have any very
    1107              :                          * good model of how planning cost compares to execution costs.
    1108              :                          * The current multiplier of 1000 * cpu_operator_cost is probably
    1109              :                          * on the low side, but we'll try this for awhile before making a
    1110              :                          * more aggressive correction.
    1111              :                          *
    1112              :                          * If we ever do write a more complicated estimator, it should
    1113              :                          * probably live in src/backend/optimizer/ not here.
    1114              :                          */
    1115           26 :                         int                     nrelations = list_length(plannedstmt->rtable);
    1116              : 
    1117           26 :                         result += 1000.0 * cpu_operator_cost * (nrelations + 1);
    1118              :                 }
    1119              :         }
    1120              : 
    1121           56 :         return result;
    1122              : }

    1124              : /*

    1252              : /*

    1285              : /*
    1286              :  * CachedPlanAllowsSimpleValidityCheck: can we use CachedPlanIsSimplyValid?
    1287              :  *
    1288              :  * This function, together with CachedPlanIsSimplyValid, provides a fast path
    1289              :  * for revalidating "simple" generic plans.  The core requirement to be simple
    1290              :  * is that the plan must not require taking any locks, which translates to
    1291              :  * not touching any tables; this happens to match up well with an important
    1292              :  * use-case in PL/pgSQL.  This function tests whether that's true, along
    1293              :  * with checking some other corner cases that we'd rather not bother with
    1294              :  * handling in the fast path.  (Note that it's still possible for such a plan
    1295              :  * to be invalidated, for example due to a change in a function that was
    1296              :  * inlined into the plan.)
    1297              :  *
    1298              :  * If the plan is simply valid, and "owner" is not NULL, record a refcount on
    1299              :  * the plan in that resowner before returning.  It is caller's responsibility
    1300              :  * to be sure that a refcount is held on any plan that's being actively used.
    1301              :  *
    1302              :  * This must only be called on known-valid generic plans (eg, ones just
    1303              :  * returned by GetCachedPlan).  If it returns true, the caller may re-use
    1304              :  * the cached plan as long as CachedPlanIsSimplyValid returns true; that
    1305              :  * check is much cheaper than the full revalidation done by GetCachedPlan.
    1306              :  * Nonetheless, no required checks are omitted.
    1307              :  */
    1308              : bool
    1309            3 : CachedPlanAllowsSimpleValidityCheck(CachedPlanSource *plansource,
    1310              :                                                                         CachedPlan *plan, ResourceOwner owner)
    1311              : {
    1312              :         ListCell   *lc;
    1313              : 
    1314              :         /*
    1315              :          * Sanity-check that the caller gave us a validated generic plan.  Notice
    1316              :          * that we *don't* assert plansource->is_valid as you might expect; that's
    1317              :          * because it's possible that that's already false when GetCachedPlan
    1318              :          * returns, e.g. because ResetPlanCache happened partway through.  We
    1319              :          * should accept the plan as long as plan->is_valid is true, and expect to
    1320              :          * replan after the next CachedPlanIsSimplyValid call.
    1321              :          */
    1322              :         Assert(plansource->magic == CACHEDPLANSOURCE_MAGIC);
    1323              :         Assert(plan->magic == CACHEDPLAN_MAGIC);
    1324              :         Assert(plan->is_valid);
    1325              :         Assert(plan == plansource->gplan);
    1326              :         Assert(plansource->search_path != NULL);
    1327              :         Assert(OverrideSearchPathMatchesCurrent(plansource->search_path));
    1328              : 
    1329              :         /* We don't support oneshot plans here. */
    1330            3 :         if (plansource->is_oneshot)
    1331            0 :                 return false;
    1332              :         Assert(!plan->is_oneshot);
    1333              : 
    1334              :         /*

    1336              :          * reject.  These things probably can't ever happen for table-free
    1337              :          * queries, but for safety's sake let's check.
    1338              :          */

    1340            0 :                 return false;
    1341            3 :         if (plan->dependsOnRole)
    1342            0 :                 return false;

    1344            0 :                 return false;
    1345              : 
    1346              :         /*
    1347              :          * Reject if AcquirePlannerLocks would have anything to do.  This is
    1348              :          * simplistic, but there's no need to inquire any more carefully; indeed,
    1349              :          * for current callers it shouldn't even be possible to hit any of these
    1350              :          * checks.
    1351              :          */
    1352            6 :         foreach(lc, plansource->query_list)
    1353              :         {
    1354            3 :                 Query      *query = lfirst_node(Query, lc);
    1355              : 
    1356            3 :                 if (query->commandType == CMD_UTILITY)
    1357            0 :                         return false;
    1358            3 :                 if (query->rtable || query->cteList || query->hasSubLinks)
    1359            0 :                         return false;
    1360              :         }
    1361              : 
    1362              :         /*

    1365              :          */

    1367              :         {
    1368            3 :                 PlannedStmt *plannedstmt = lfirst_node(PlannedStmt, lc);
    1369              :                 ListCell   *lc2;
    1370              : 
    1371            3 :                 if (plannedstmt->commandType == CMD_UTILITY)
    1372            0 :                         return false;
    1373              : 
    1374              :                 /*

    1379              :                 {

    1382            3 :                         if (rte->rtekind == RTE_RELATION)
    1383            0 :                                 return false;
    1384              :                 }
    1385              :         }
    1386              : 
    1387              :         /*

    1391              : 
    1392              :         /* Bump refcount if requested. */

    1402              : 
    1403              : /*
    1404              :  * CachedPlanIsSimplyValid: quick check for plan still being valid
    1405              :  *
    1406              :  * This function must not be used unless CachedPlanAllowsSimpleValidityCheck
    1407              :  * previously said it was OK.
    1408              :  *
    1409              :  * If the plan is valid, and "owner" is not NULL, record a refcount on
    1410              :  * the plan in that resowner before returning.  It is caller's responsibility
    1411              :  * to be sure that a refcount is held on any plan that's being actively used.
    1412              :  *
    1413              :  * The code here is unconditionally safe as long as the only use of this
    1414              :  * CachedPlanSource is in connection with the particular CachedPlan pointer
    1415              :  * that's passed in.  If the plansource were being used for other purposes,
    1416              :  * it's possible that its generic plan could be invalidated and regenerated
    1417              :  * while the current caller wasn't looking, and then there could be a chance
    1418              :  * collision of address between this caller's now-stale plan pointer and the
    1419              :  * actual address of the new generic plan.  For current uses, that scenario
    1420              :  * can't happen; but with a plansource shared across multiple uses, it'd be
    1421              :  * advisable to also save plan->generation and verify that that still matches.
    1422              :  */
    1423              : bool
    1424            6 : CachedPlanIsSimplyValid(CachedPlanSource *plansource, CachedPlan *plan,
    1425              :                                                 ResourceOwner owner)
    1426              : {
    1427              :         /*
    1428              :          * Careful here: since the caller doesn't necessarily hold a refcount on
    1429              :          * the plan to start with, it's possible that "plan" is a dangling
    1430              :          * pointer.  Don't dereference it until we've verified that it still
    1431              :          * matches the plansource's gplan (which is either valid or NULL).
    1432              :          */
    1433              :         Assert(plansource->magic == CACHEDPLANSOURCE_MAGIC);
    1434              : 
    1435              :         /*
    1436              :          * Has cache invalidation fired on this plan?  We can check this right
    1437              :          * away since there are no locks that we'd need to acquire first.  Note
    1438              :          * that here we *do* check plansource->is_valid, so as to force plan
    1439              :          * rebuild if that's become false.
    1440              :          */
    1441            6 :         if (!plansource->is_valid ||
    1442            6 :                 plan == NULL || plan != plansource->gplan ||
    1443            6 :                 !plan->is_valid)
    1444            0 :                 return false;
    1445              : 
    1446              :         Assert(plan->magic == CACHEDPLAN_MAGIC);
    1447              : 
    1448              :         /* Is the search_path still the same as when we made it? */
    1449              :         Assert(plansource->search_path != NULL);
    1450            6 :         if (!OverrideSearchPathMatchesCurrent(plansource->search_path))
    1451            0 :                 return false;
    1452              : 
    1453              :         /* It's still good.  Bump refcount if requested. */
    1454            6 :         if (owner)
    1455              :         {
    1456            0 :                 ResourceOwnerEnlargePlanCacheRefs(owner);
    1457            0 :                 plan->refcount++;
    1458            0 :                 ResourceOwnerRememberPlanCacheRef(owner, plan);
    1459              :         }
    1460              : 
    1461            6 :         return true;
    1462              : }
    1463              : 
    1464              : /*
    1465              :  * CachedPlanSetParentContext: move a CachedPlanSource to a new memory context
    1466              :  *
    1467              :  * This can only be applied to unsaved plans; once saved, a plan always
    1468              :  * lives underneath CacheMemoryContext.
    1469              :  */
    1470              : void
    1471           30 : CachedPlanSetParentContext(CachedPlanSource *plansource,
    1472              :                                                    MemoryContext newcontext)
    1473              : {
    1474              :         /* Assert caller is doing things in a sane order */
    1475              :         Assert(plansource->magic == CACHEDPLANSOURCE_MAGIC);
    1476              :         Assert(plansource->is_complete);
    1477              : 
    1478              :         /* These seem worth real tests, though */
    1479           30 :         if (plansource->is_saved)
    1480            0 :                 elog(ERROR, "cannot move a saved cached plan to another context");
    1481           30 :         if (plansource->is_oneshot)
    1482            0 :                 elog(ERROR, "cannot move a one-shot cached plan to another context");
    1483              : 
    1484              :         /* OK, let the caller keep the plan where he wishes */
    1485           30 :         MemoryContextSetParent(plansource->context, newcontext);
    1486              : 
    1487              :         /*
    1488              :          * The query_context needs no special handling, since it's a child of
    1489              :          * plansource->context.  But if there's a generic plan, it should be
    1490              :          * maintained as a sibling of plansource->context.
    1491              :          */
    1492           30 :         if (plansource->gplan)

    1499              : /*

    1820              : /*

    1840           67 :                         case RTE_RELATION:

    1848            0 :                         case RTE_SUBQUERY:

    1854              :                                 /* ignore other types of RTEs */

    1879              : /*

    1940              : /*
    1941              :  * PlanCacheRelCallback
    1942              :  *              Relcache inval callback function
    1943              :  *
    1944              :  * Invalidate all plans mentioning the given rel, or all plans mentioning

    1946              :  */
    1947              : static void
    1948         5273 : PlanCacheRelCallback(Datum arg, Oid relid)
    1949              : {

    1956              :

    1961        24782 :                         continue;
    1962              :

    2015              :

    2146              : /*
    2147              :  * ResetPlanCache: invalidate all cached plans.
    2148              :  */
    2149              : void
    2150           38 : ResetPlanCache(void)
    2151              : {
    2152              :         dlist_iter      iter;
    2153              : 
    2154          166 :         dlist_foreach(iter, &saved_plan_list)
    2155              :         {
    2156          128 :                 CachedPlanSource *plansource = dlist_container(CachedPlanSource,
    2157              :                                                                                                            node, iter.cur);

    2159              :                 Assert(plansource->magic == CACHEDPLANSOURCE_MAGIC);
    2160              : 
    2161              :                 /* No work if it's already invalidated */
    2162          128 :                 if (!plansource->is_valid)
    2163          119 :                         continue;
    2164              : 
    2165              :                 /*
    2166              :                  * We *must not* mark transaction control statements as invalid,
    2167              :                  * particularly not ROLLBACK, because they may need to be executed in
    2168              :                  * aborted transactions when we can't revalidate them (cf bug #5269).
    2169              :                  * In general there's no point in invalidating statements for which a
    2170              :                  * new parse analysis/rewrite/plan cycle would certainly give the same
    2171              :                  * results.
    2172              :                  */
